    • clientOrderId

      public OptionOrder.Builder clientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique option order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientOrderId

      public OptionOrder.Builder clientOrderId(Optional<String> clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique option order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientReceivedTime

      public OptionOrder.Builder clientReceivedTime(OffsetDateTime clientReceivedTime)
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

      public OptionOrder.Builder clientReceivedTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime)
      Required for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf.

    • createTime

      public OptionOrder.Builder createTime(OffsetDateTime createTime)
      Time of the option order creation

    • createTime

      public OptionOrder.Builder createTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> createTime)
      Time of the option order creation

    • cumulativeNotionalValue

      public OptionOrder.Builder cumulativeNotionalValue(OptionOrderCumulativeNotionalValue cumulativeNotionalValue)
      This represents the absolute value of the notional amount (without fees) transacted across all of this legs. If present, this will always a positive number, or zero. This field will be empty if there are no leg executions.

    • cumulativeNotionalValue

      public OptionOrder.Builder cumulativeNotionalValue(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderCumulativeNotionalValue> cumulativeNotionalValue)
      This represents the absolute value of the notional amount (without fees) transacted across all of this legs. If present, this will always a positive number, or zero. This field will be empty if there are no leg executions.

    • cumulativeNotionalValueDirection

      public OptionOrder.Builder cumulativeNotionalValueDirection(CumulativeNotionalValueDirection cumulativeNotionalValueDirection)
      This represents the direction of the total filled notional value, which will be present if the `cumulative_notional_value` is also present. If there are no executions, this value will be absent. When the summed notional value of all CREDIT legs exceeds that of the DEBIT legs a CREDIT will be reported here; otherwise a DEBIT will be reported.

    • cumulativeNotionalValueDirection

      public OptionOrder.Builder cumulativeNotionalValueDirection(Optional<? extends CumulativeNotionalValueDirection> cumulativeNotionalValueDirection)
      This represents the direction of the total filled notional value, which will be present if the `cumulative_notional_value` is also present. If there are no executions, this value will be absent. When the summed notional value of all CREDIT legs exceeds that of the DEBIT legs a CREDIT will be reported here; otherwise a DEBIT will be reported.

    • currencyCode

      public OptionOrder.Builder currencyCode(String currencyCode)
      Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • currencyCode

      public OptionOrder.Builder currencyCode(Optional<String> currencyCode)
      Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • fees

      public OptionOrder.Builder fees(List<TradingFee> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

    • fees

      public OptionOrder.Builder fees(Optional<? extends List<TradingFee>> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this option order.

    • lastUpdateTime

      public OptionOrder.Builder lastUpdateTime(OffsetDateTime lastUpdateTime)
      Time of the last option order update

    • lastUpdateTime

      public OptionOrder.Builder lastUpdateTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> lastUpdateTime)
      Time of the last option order update

    • legs

      public OptionOrder.Builder legs(List<OptionOrderLeg> legs)
      The legs of this option order. Only a single leg is allowed to be specified, and it must be for an OPTION security.

    • legs

      public OptionOrder.Builder legs(Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderLeg>> legs)
      The legs of this option order. Only a single leg is allowed to be specified, and it must be for an OPTION security.

    • limitPrice

      public OptionOrder.Builder limitPrice(OptionOrderLimitPrice limitPrice)
      The limit price for this option order. For single-leg option orders, this is the price per contract which the filled order must match or beat. For multi-leg orders, this represents the amortized "price per unit" that must be matched or beat. (E.g. with a limit price of a $4 DEBIT, and an order with 2 legs that includes one leg buy 100 shares of SBX and another leg to sell 1 contract of SBX, then the fills will be guaranteed to result in no more than a $400--4 USD X 100 shares/contract--total debit against the ordering account.)

    • limitPrice

      public OptionOrder.Builder limitPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderLimitPrice> limitPrice)
      The limit price for this option order. For single-leg option orders, this is the price per contract which the filled order must match or beat. For multi-leg orders, this represents the amortized "price per unit" that must be matched or beat. (E.g. with a limit price of a $4 DEBIT, and an order with 2 legs that includes one leg buy 100 shares of SBX and another leg to sell 1 contract of SBX, then the fills will be guaranteed to result in no more than a $400--4 USD X 100 shares/contract--total debit against the ordering account.)

    • name

      public OptionOrder.Builder name(String name)
      System generated name of the option order.

    • name

      public OptionOrder.Builder name(Optional<String> name)
      System generated name of the option order.

    • optionOrderId

      public OptionOrder.Builder optionOrderId(String optionOrderId)
      System generated unique id for the option order.

    • optionOrderId

      public OptionOrder.Builder optionOrderId(Optional<String> optionOrderId)
      System generated unique id for the option order.

    • orderDate

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderDate(OptionOrderOrderDate orderDate)
      The date on which the option order will go to the market: must be "today". Option orders will only be accepted during normal trading hours.

    • orderDate

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderDate(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderOrderDate> orderDate)
      The date on which the option order will go to the market: must be "today". Option orders will only be accepted during normal trading hours.

    • orderRejectedReason

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderRejectedReason(OptionOrderOrderRejectedReason orderRejectedReason)
      When an option order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • orderRejectedReason

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderRejectedReason(Optional<? extends OptionOrderOrderRejectedReason> orderRejectedReason)
      When an option order has the REJECTED status, this will be populated with a system code describing the rejection.

    • orderStatus

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderStatus(OptionOrderOrderStatus orderStatus)
      The processing status of the option order

    • orderStatus

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderStatus(Optional<? extends OptionOrderOrderStatus> orderStatus)
      The processing status of the option order

    • orderType

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderType(OptionOrderOrderType orderType)
      The execution type of this option order.

    • orderType

      public OptionOrder.Builder orderType(Optional<? extends OptionOrderOrderType> orderType)
      The execution type of this option order.

    • priceDirection

      public OptionOrder.Builder priceDirection(OptionOrderPriceDirection priceDirection)
      The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    • priceDirection

      public OptionOrder.Builder priceDirection(Optional<? extends OptionOrderPriceDirection> priceDirection)
      The direction of the price, which indicates whether the entire option order should result in a credit to the account placing the order, or a debit from the account placing the order.

    • quantity

      public OptionOrder.Builder quantity(OptionOrderQuantity quantity)
      The quantity of the option order to be placed. The `ratio_quantity` of each leg will be multiplied by this value to determine the total number of units being ordered within each leg. (E.g. if a quantity of 3 is specified, and a leg with a `ratio_quantity` of 2 is specified, then the total number of contracts being ordered by that leg will be 6.)

    • quantity

      public OptionOrder.Builder quantity(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OptionOrderQuantity> quantity)
      The quantity of the option order to be placed. The `ratio_quantity` of each leg will be multiplied by this value to determine the total number of units being ordered within each leg. (E.g. if a quantity of 3 is specified, and a leg with a `ratio_quantity` of 2 is specified, then the total number of contracts being ordered by that leg will be 6.)

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public OptionOrder.Builder specialReportingInstructions(List<OptionOrderSpecialReportingInstructions> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public OptionOrder.Builder specialReportingInstructions(Optional<? extends List<OptionOrderSpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions)
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this option order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • timeInForce

      public OptionOrder.Builder timeInForce(OptionOrderTimeInForce timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate the system captures the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • timeInForce

      public OptionOrder.Builder timeInForce(Optional<? extends OptionOrderTimeInForce> timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate the system captures the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • build

      public OptionOrder build()
